tiles.name: Old Kent Road
tiles.funFact: One of London's oldest roads, dating back to Roman times when it connected London to Canterbury!

tiles.name: Whitechapel Road
tiles.funFact: Historic East End street, famous for the Whitechapel Bell Foundry which cast the Liberty Bell!

tiles.name: King's Cross Station
tiles.funFact: Platform 9¾ at King's Cross is where Harry Potter catches the Hogwarts Express!

tiles.name: The Angel Islington
tiles.funFact: Named after a historic coaching inn, The Angel has been a London landmark since 1614!

tiles.name: Euston Road
tiles.funFact: Home to the British Library, which houses over 170 million items including the Magna Carta!

tiles.name: Pentonville Road
tiles.funFact: Named after Henry Penton who developed the area in the 1770s, near historic Pentonville Prison.

tiles.name: Pall Mall
tiles.funFact: Home to exclusive gentlemen's clubs since the 1800s, and the famous Royal Automobile Club!

tiles.name: Electric Company
tiles.funFact: London's first power station opened in 1882, bringing electric street lights to the city!

tiles.name: Whitehall
tiles.funFact: The center of UK government! Home to 10 Downing Street and numerous ministry buildings.

tiles.name: Northumberland Ave
tiles.funFact: Built in the 1870s, this grand avenue connects Trafalgar Square to the Thames Embankment!

tiles.name: Marylebone Station
tiles.funFact: London's most recent major station, opened in 1899, and featured in many Sherlock Holmes stories!

tiles.name: Bow Street
tiles.funFact: Home to the Royal Opera House and the first professional police force, the Bow Street Runners!

tiles.name: Marlborough Street
tiles.funFact: Located in Mayfair, this elegant street has been home to British aristocracy for centuries!

tiles.name: Vine Street
tiles.funFact: A prestigious Mayfair address, Vine Street has housed famous residents and luxury establishments!

tiles.name: Strand
tiles.funFact: One of London's busiest thoroughfares, the Strand connects Westminster to the City of London!

tiles.name: Fleet Street
tiles.funFact: The historic heart of British journalism! Named after the River Fleet that runs beneath it.

tiles.name: Trafalgar Square
tiles.funFact: Named after the Battle of Trafalgar, features Nelson's Column and is visited by 15 million people yearly!

tiles.name: Fenchurch St Station
tiles.funFact: The smallest of London's major terminals, built in 1841, serving the historic City of London!

tiles.name: Leicester Square
tiles.funFact: The heart of London's entertainment district, hosting movie premieres and surrounded by theaters!

tiles.name: Coventry Street
tiles.funFact: Connects Piccadilly Circus to Leicester Square, lined with restaurants and entertainment venues!

tiles.name: Water Works
tiles.funFact: London's water comes from the Thames and Lea rivers, treating over 2.6 billion liters daily!

tiles.name: Piccadilly
tiles.funFact: Home to Piccadilly Circus and luxury shopping! Named after piccadills, fashionable 17th century collars.

tiles.name: Regent Street
tiles.funFact: Designed by John Nash in 1811, Regent Street curves elegantly through the West End with flagship stores!

tiles.name: Oxford Street
tiles.funFact: Europe's busiest shopping street with over 300 shops and 200 million visitors each year!

tiles.name: Bond Street
tiles.funFact: The epitome of luxury since the 1700s, home to world-famous jewelers, fashion houses, and art dealers!

tiles.name: Liverpool St Station
tiles.funFact: One of London's busiest stations, serving over 67 million passengers annually!

tiles.name: Park Lane
tiles.funFact: Overlooks Hyde Park and is home to some of London's most expensive real estate and luxury hotels!

tiles.name: Mayfair
tiles.funFact: London's most exclusive district! Named after the annual May Fair held here from 1686 to 1764.
